failure_modes.why: RED never upgraded to cities, dumping ore via maritime trades instead of converting to VP.
RED churned dozens of 3:1 maritime trades all game without building meaningful VP-producing structures.
RED stopped at its initial settlements, building only roads and never adding settlement/city VP.

review_summary: RED stalled at 2 settlements and frittered resources on endless maritime trades instead of upgrading to cities or expanding, leaving no path to 10 VP.

0.summary: Node 25 is a fine opening placement with zero oracle regret across all criteria.

50.summary: BLUE's robber move onto Red's high-value tile to steal is the optimal, zero-regret choice.

152.summary: BLUE correctly moves the robber onto an opponent-adjacent producing tile, a costless and tempo-positive blocking play with no regret.

255.summary: A reasonable maritime conversion of surplus ORE into BRICK to enable an immediate expansion road, defensible despite being made from a weak position.

455.summary: RED correctly uses the robber to hit the runaway Blue leader, a fine no-regret robber placement.

356.summary: A harmless but uninfluential robber move with no steal that fails to meaningfully slow the leading BLUE player.

532.summary: A reasonable surplus-sheep conversion to wheat; wood was an equally valid pick given zero wood but wheat is defensible.

652.summary: Optimal robber placement targeting RED matches the oracle's best move with zero regret.

587.summary: A reasonable surplus-ore-to-brick bank trade that supports continued road building while leading.

712.summary: A sensible 3:1 maritime trade converting surplus brick into scarce wheat to enable a build, with no downside.

833.summary: A defensible 2:1 port trade for brick to fuel a build, with no opponent benefit but limited upside given the boxed-in board.

778.summary: A defensible 2:1 ore-for-brick bank trade, slightly off the oracle's wheat preference but no real mistake.

953.summary: Optimal 3:1 trade of surplus wheat for scarce brick to enable building, matching the oracle.

886.summary: A reasonable surplus-WOOD bank trade, though converting to WHEAT (which the hand lacked) was marginally better than SHEEP.

1034.summary: Optimal robber placement on BLUE matching the oracle's best legal move with no regret.
